records.effective_text_en: A relevant species is one in which the test material is pharmacologically active due to the expression of the receptor or an epitope. A number of factors should be taken into account when determining species relevancy: comparisons of target sequence homology between species can be an appropriate starting point, followed by in vitro assays for qualitative and quantitative cross-species comparisons of relative target binding affinities and receptor/ligand occupancy and kinetics, and assessments of functional activity are also recommended.
records.synthesis_rationale: Candidate amendment-synthesis slice built from the clarifies mapping slice.s6.amend.001. Parent record ich_s6_r1.kr.part1.3_3.002 supplies the operative definition of a relevant species; ich_s6_r1.kr.part1.3_3.003 supplies the general statement that a variety of techniques can identify one. Addendum records ich_s6_r1.kr.part2.2_1.001 through .003 supply the specific factors and techniques (sequence homology, in vitro binding/occupancy comparisons, functional-activity assessment) used to satisfy that Parent definition in practice. This reconciles, for the same real endpoint pair, with the reviewed Module 3.3 mapping ich_s6_r1.amend.001 (REV-005): both reach the clarifies conclusion that the Addendum explains and disambiguates evidence for the Parent's relevant-species definition without changing it. No unexplained semantic divergence from that frozen reference is present.
